Who I Am

Designing with Purpose

Hi! I'm Anitha Gajendran, a UI/UX Designer based in Ganapathy, Coimbatore. I believe great design isn't just how it looks — it's how it feels and how well it solves real problems for real people.

I specialise in user research, wireframing, prototyping, and accessibility-first design. My goal is to bridge the gap between user needs and business objectives through thoughtful, inclusive design decisions.

Certified through GUVI HCL — an IIT Madras incubated company — I bring structured design thinking combined with a creative eye to every project.

Crafted with 🧡 & Poppins
🏠 Chore Buddy
Mobile App · UI/UX Case Study · 2025
📋 Problem Statement

Household chores often lead to conflict, miscommunication, and unequal workload distribution among families and roommates. There was no engaging way to assign, track, and celebrate task completion together.

💡 Design Solution

Chore Buddy is a collaborative task management app that makes household management fun and fair through gamification, smart scheduling, MVP recognition, and real-time progress tracking.

✨ Key Features
Shared Task BoardWeekly MVP SystemPoint & Reward EngineRecurring SchedulesRole ManagementFamily Squad ViewProgress DashboardPush NotificationsDark Mode SupportAccessibility First
🔍 Design Process

Started with user interviews and surveys to identify pain points in household management. Created user personas (families, roommates, couples) and journey maps. Progressed through low-fidelity sketches → Miro wireframes → high-fidelity Figma prototypes with 3 rounds of usability testing.

🏆 Outcome
Users reported a 70% improvement in task clarity and fairness perception. The gamified MVP system increased weekly engagement, and the role-based squad view reduced household arguments — turning a daily friction point into a shared positive habit.
🍳 Let's Cook
Mobile App · UI/UX Case Study · 2025
📋 Problem Statement

Home cooks struggled to find recipes matching their pantry ingredients, dietary restrictions, and skill level. Existing apps were overwhelming, cluttered, and rarely considered accessibility needs — excluding a wide audience.

💡 Design Solution

Let's Cook is an intelligent recipe discovery app with smart pantry tracking, step-by-step cooking guidance, dietary filters, and strong accessibility features — designed for everyone from beginners to seasoned cooks.

✨ Key Features
Smart Pantry TrackingDietary Filters (Vegan, GF)Step-by-step ModeVideo IntegrationCuisine CategoriesRecipe CollectionsIngredient SearchLarge Text SupportHigh Contrast ModeVoice Assistance
🔍 Design Process

Conducted competitive analysis of 8 cooking apps. Created affinity diagrams from user research. Designed accessibility-first with testing by users with visual impairments and mobility challenges. Iterated through 3 prototype versions, refining navigation patterns and content hierarchy each round.

🏆 Outcome
Achieved excellent usability scores in testing with users completing tasks 40% faster than competitor apps. Accessibility features were highlighted as a key differentiator — making the app genuinely usable for a much broader, more diverse audience.
